question: Find the simple interest on a principal of ₹1000 at an interest rate of 10% per year for a duration of 2 years.

A sum of money is divided among A, B, and C for 2, 3, and 4 years respectively at 5% simple interest per annum such that they receive the same interest amount. What is the investment ratio of A : B : C?

A sum of Rs. 2500 is split and lent out into two parts: one at 12% simple interest per annum and the other at 10% per annum. If the total annual interest earned is Rs. 290, find the amount lent at 10%.
The difference between the simple interest on a certain sum at 10% per annum for 2 years and compound interest compounded every 6 months is Rs. 124.05. What is the principal sum?

X was supposed to pay back Rs. 23,000 at the end of 3 years at 5% simple interest. He makes two periodic payments: Rs. 9,450 at the end of Year 1 and Rs. 4,400 at the end of Year 2. Find the remaining balance at the end of the third year.

options.B: Rs. 9,150 (on original principal) / Rs. 8,114 (on declining balance)
options.C: Rs. 9,850 (on original principal) / Rs. 8,414 (on declining balance)
